zh.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland於大約1363年出生在蘇格蘭金加迪爾。他於1379年與Matilda Urquhart在英國蘇格蘭費伏結婚。他於1380年住在英國蘇格蘭安格斯福爾法爾。他去世於1430年7月20日在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾登諾塔享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Keith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the lands of Keith in East Lothian. The placename is derived from British Celtic cait- ‘wood’. In the 17th century numerous bearers of this name settled in Ulster.
German: nickname from Middle High German kīt ‘sprout, offspring’.

History: George Keith (c. 1638–1716), born at Peterhead, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, came to NJ in or before 1685. In 1689 he settled in Philadelphia, where he became headmaster of the school now called the William Penn Charter School. He came into sharp collision with the Quaker leaders in PA and formed a separatist party known as the Christian Quakers, popularly known as ‘Keithians’.

variants.name: Kight
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Kite  .

variants.name: Keath
meanings: Scottish and English (mainly Cambridgeshire): variant of   Keith  .

variants.name: Kite
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English kete, kyte ‘kite’ (the bird of prey), perhaps a nickname for a rapacious person.
English: habitational name from one or more of the minor places with names deriving from Old English cȳte, cyte ‘cottage, hut, shed’, such as Kite Farm in Swalecliffe (Kent).

variants.name: Keeth
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Keith  .

variants.name: Keitt
meanings: Possibly an altered form of German   Keith   2.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland was born about 1363, in Kincardineshire, Scotland. He married Matilda Urquhart in 1379, in Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom. He lived in Forfar, Angus, Scotland, United Kingdom in 1380. He died on 20 July 1430, in Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotlandは約1363年にスコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアで生まれた。1379年にイギリススコットランドフィフでMatilda Urquhartと結婚した。彼は1380年にはイギリススコットランドアンガスフォーファルに住んでいた。彼は1430年7月20日にイギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアダノッタルで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland(은)는 대략 1363년 스컷랜드 킨카딘실르에서 태어났다. 그는 1379년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프에서 Matilda Urquhart(와)과 결혼했다. 1380년도에 영국 스코틀랜드 앤구스 포파르에서 살았다. 그는 1430년 7월 20일 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르 던타르에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland родился около 1363 г. в местности кинкардаинешир, Шотландия. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Urquhart в 1379 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания. Он жил в местности форфар, ангус, Шотландия, Великобритания в 1380 году. Он умер 20 июля 1430 г. в местности дунноттар, кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет.
